title: Khmer sculpture
highlights:
Châmleăk Khmêr [[cɑmˈlak kʰmae]]) is the stone sculpture of the Khmer Empire, which ruled a territory based on modern Cambodia, but rather larger, from the 9th to the 13th century. 2. Angkor Wat style (1100–1175) 3. Baphuon style (1010–1080)
4. Koh Ker style (941–944)
